seoTitle: How to Add Logic in Typeform for Smarter Forms
metaDescription: Learn how to add conditional logic in Typeform to create branching paths, skip questions, and personalize the respondent experience.
intro: A form that adapts to each respondent feels more relevant and takes less time to complete. This guide covers how to add logic in Typeform to create branching paths, skip unnecessary questions, and personalize the experience based on how someone answers.

steps.heading: Open the Logic Builder
steps.body: Access the Logic section in the Typeform editor to start setting up rules for how your form should adapt.

steps.heading: Create branching rules
steps.body: Define conditions that determine which question comes next based on a respondent's previous answer.

steps.heading: Test the logic flow
steps.body: Preview the form and try different answer paths to confirm the branching behaves the way you intended.

faq.question: What can logic be used for in Typeform?
faq.answer: Logic can create conditional branching for surveys, quizzes, lead generation forms, customer feedback, registrations, and applications.

faq.question: Does adding logic slow down the form for respondents?
faq.answer: No, logic runs instantly as respondents answer, and it typically shortens the form by skipping irrelevant questions.

faq.question: Can I combine multiple logic rules in one form?
faq.answer: Yes, you can set up multiple branching rules across different questions to build a more personalized path through the form.

faq.question: Do I need to test logic before publishing?
faq.answer: Yes, testing different answer combinations helps confirm the branching paths work correctly before respondents start using the live form.
